name: Compiled Obsidian Energy Metering Knowledge Base

entries.name: ABNT NBR 14519
entries.definition: Brazilian standard referenced for electronic meter interface characteristics such as optical communication.

entries.name: ABNT NBR 14522
entries.definition: Brazilian standard commonly referenced for electronic energy meter communication and data handling.

entries.name: Active Energy
entries.definition: Measured active energy, usually expressed in Wh or kWh.

entries.name: Activity Calendar
entries.definition: Calendar and special day configuration used for tariff periods and billing behavior.

entries.name: AES-GCM
entries.definition: Authenticated encryption algorithm referenced in DLMS/COSEM security suites.

entries.name: Alarm
entries.definition: Meter indication for abnormal conditions that may need attention.

entries.name: Association
entries.definition: Logical connection between a DLMS/COSEM client and server with defined access rights and security level.

entries.name: Billing Profile
entries.definition: Profile storing metering quantities related to billing periods.

entries.name: Blue Book Part 1 OBIS
entries.definition: DLMS UA Blue Book Part 1 defines the OBIS object identification system used to identify COSEM object instances, measured data, abstract data, display codes, and medium-specific data items.
scope: relation between OBIS logical names and COSEM objects
entries.runtime_use: Use this entry to recognize requirements that cite the Blue Book OBIS object identification system or IEC 62056-6-1.

entries.name: Blue Book Part 2 COSEM Interface Classes
entries.definition: DLMS UA Blue Book Part 2 defines COSEM interface classes, including class identifiers, versions, attributes, methods, access semantics, and the relationship between interface classes and OBIS logical names.
entries.runtime_use: Use this entry to recognize requirements that cite the Blue Book COSEM interface class catalogue or IEC 62056-6-2.

entries.name: Bypass
entries.definition: Irregularity or tamper-related condition detected by the meter.

entries.name: Clock
entries.definition: Meter clock and calendar function used for time-based records and tariff periods.

entries.name: COSEM
entries.definition: Object model used with DLMS for representing metering data, configuration, and control functions.

entries.name: Current
entries.definition: Electrical current measurement.

entries.name: Disconnect Control
entries.definition: Meter function for remote or local disconnection and reconnection control.

entries.name: DLMS
entries.definition: Protocol suite used for utility meter data exchange and device management.

entries.name: ECDH
entries.definition: Key agreement method used in DLMS/COSEM security suites.

entries.name: ECDSA
entries.definition: Digital signature algorithm used in DLMS/COSEM security suites.

entries.name: Error
entries.definition: Meter record for exceptional or failed operations.

entries.name: Event
entries.definition: Meter log entry representing occurrence of a defined operational, security, quality, or irregularity event.

entries.name: Firmware Update
entries.definition: Process or object set related to updating meter firmware and recording firmware changes.

entries.name: HDLC
entries.definition: Data link protocol used by some DLMS/COSEM communication profiles.

entries.name: High Level Security
entries.definition: DLMS/COSEM high-level authentication mechanism.

entries.name: IEC 62056
entries.definition: International standard family for electricity metering data exchange, including DLMS/COSEM application layer, OBIS, and interface classes.

entries.name: Load Profile
entries.definition: Time-series profile for measured or calculated quantities over configured intervals.

entries.name: Logical Device
entries.definition: Abstract entity inside a physical meter that exposes functionality through COSEM objects.

entries.name: LoRaWAN
entries.definition: Low-power wide-area network profile sometimes used for remote metering.

entries.name: Low Level Security
entries.definition: DLMS/COSEM low-level authentication mechanism, commonly password-based.

entries.name: LTE CAT-M
entries.definition: Cellular communication technologies and network interface cards used for remote metering.

entries.name: OBIS
entries.definition: Code system used to identify measured quantities and meter data objects.

entries.name: PLC-PRIME
entries.definition: Power-line communication profile for smart metering networks.

entries.name: QEE
entries.definition: Energy or power quality indicators and measurements.

entries.name: Reactive Energy
entries.definition: Measured reactive energy or related reactive quantities.

entries.name: Register
entries.definition: Meter data object used to store measured energy, demand, or related values.

entries.name: RTM 587/2012
entries.definition: Brazilian metrological regulation often referenced for electricity meters.

entries.name: SAP
entries.definition: Service access point identifier for DLMS/COSEM clients and logical devices.

entries.name: Smart Meter
entries.definition: Electricity meter with advanced measurement, communication, event logging, and remote management functions.

entries.name: SMF
entries.definition: Billing measurement system used in regulated electricity metering contexts.

entries.name: Snapshot
entries.definition: Recorded instantaneous or captured values from meter objects.

entries.name: Voltage
entries.definition: Electrical potential measurement, often phase or line related.

entries.name: Wi-SUN
entries.definition: Wireless communication profile used in smart utility networks.

entries.name: Association LN
entries.definition: COSEM object representing the current logical-name association, including object list, partners, context, authentication, and security setup references.

entries.name: Billing Profile
entries.definition: Profile and object group for storing energy and demand quantities used for billing periods.

entries.name: Blue Book Interface Class Catalogue
entries.definition: The Blue Book Part 2 interface class catalogue groups COSEM interface classes by purpose, including measurement data, access control, time and event control, payment, communication profile setup, Internet setup, PLC setup, ZigBee, Wi-SUN, and relation to OBIS objects.

entries.name: COSEM Access Rights Model
entries.definition: Model for defining whether each client role can read attributes, write attributes, or invoke methods on COSEM objects.

client_columns.meaning: service/local or specialized client depending on table context

entries.name: COSEM Logical Device Name
entries.definition: COSEM data object containing the unique logical device identification.

entries.name: COSEM Object Model
entries.definition: COSEM objects are instances of interface classes identified by logical names and defined by attributes, methods, access rights, and data types.

entries.name: Device ID 1
entries.definition: COSEM data object containing the electronic meter serial number.

entries.name: Disconnect Control Scheduler
entries.definition: COSEM schedule object used to trigger disconnect and reconnect scripts.

key_attributes.name: executed_script
key_attributes.meaning: script identifier for disconnect or reconnect

entries.name: DLMS/COSEM Communication Profiles
entries.definition: Communication profiles considered for the DLMS/COSEM smart meter profile.

profiles.name: HDLC
profiles.medium: data link / serial or IP-related DLMS profile context

entries.name: DLMS/COSEM Security Suites
entries.definition: Available DLMS/COSEM cryptographic suites and related algorithms.

entries.name: DLMS/COSEM Smart Meter Profile
entries.definition: Application and object profile for smart electricity meters using DLMS/COSEM services and COSEM objects.

entries.name: Event Group and Subgroup Model
entries.definition: Event model that classifies logs by group number, subgroup number, event number, and event description.

entries.name: High Level Security Association
entries.definition: Association security level using high-level authentication for management and measurement access.

entries.name: Image Transfer
entries.definition: COSEM object allowing firmware image transfer to COSEM servers.

entries.name: Load Profile
entries.definition: Time-series profile for load curve records and related measurement quantities.

entries.name: Local and Remote Association Matrix
entries.definition: Association matrix that maps client roles to management and metering logical devices and their required security level.

entries.name: Local Management and Measurement Client
entries.definition: Client role used for local access by a concentrator, collector, or central system. It generally has similar rights to the remote management client, with restrictions on remote association attributes.

entries.name: Low Level Security Association
entries.definition: Association security level typically using password-based authentication for read access.

entries.name: Management Logical Device
entries.definition: Mandatory logical device in a physical meter that exposes device management information, structure discovery, configuration, events, alarms, firmware update, and security-related objects.

entries.name: Metering Logical Device
entries.definition: Logical device that exposes the meter's measurement and billing functionality through COSEM objects.

entries.name: OBIS Logical Name
entries.definition: Logical name used to identify COSEM object instances, commonly represented as six OBIS value groups.

entries.name: OBIS Value Groups
entries.definition: OBIS logical names use six value groups in the A-B:C.D.E.F structure. The groups identify media, channels, data items, processing or classification, tariff or further processing, and historical or billing period allocation.

groups.meaning: Media or energy type; A=0 is abstract, A=1 AC electricity, A=2 DC electricity, A=4 heat cost allocator, A=5/6 thermal energy, A=7 gas, A=8 cold water, A=9 hot water.
Measurement or communication channel; B=0 means no channel specified, B=1..64 channel numbers.
Abstract or physical data item such as current, voltage, power, volume, temperature, list object, or data profile object.
Type, result of processing, or further classification for the item identified by A to C.
Tariff rate or other further classification, depending on the medium-specific object family.
Billing period, historical value, or other storage instance allocation.

standard_ranges.manufacturer_specific: Selected ranges such as 128..199 or 128..254 depending on value group and context.

entries.name: Power Quality Profile
entries.definition: Object and quantity group for power quality measurements and indicators.

entries.name: Public Client
entries.definition: Client role used to reveal the structure of an unknown metering device, typically through the management logical device without security.
limitations: No access to measurement data requiring authentication
No read or write access to password values

entries.name: Read Client
entries.definition: Client role used for reading parameters and energy data. Password access through LLS is typically required.
limitations: No read or write access to password values

entries.name: Remote Management and Measurement Client
entries.definition: Client role used by data concentrators, collectors, or central systems for remote point-to-point access to management and measurement functions.

entries.name: SAP Assignment
entries.definition: COSEM object listing logical devices and their SAP assignments.

entries.name: Security Policy Bit Map
entries.definition: Bit-level meaning of DLMS/COSEM security policy states.

bits.meaning: not used, must be set to 0
not used, must be set to 0

states.meaning: all messages are authenticated and encrypted

services.name: ACCESS selective

entries.name: Data
entries.definition: Generic COSEM interface class for a single data value.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the object

attributes.name: value
attributes.meaning: Stored data value

access_semantics: The value data type is determined by the object instance logical_name and must be chosen so the value can be interpreted unambiguously.

source_refs.section: 4.3.1 Data (class_id = 1, version = 0)

entries.name: Register
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for a measured value with scaler and unit.

methods.name: reset
methods.meaning: Reset register value when supported

common_instances.name: Time threshold for long power failure

access_semantics: A Register models a process or status value together with its associated scaler and unit; the logical_name identifies the nature of the value.

source_refs.section: 4.3.2 Register (class_id = 3, version = 0)

entries.name: Extended Register
entries.definition: COSEM register with status and capture time in addition to value and scaler/unit.
behavior_notes: Extends Register with status and capture_time so the value can be qualified by status and capture timestamp.
reset sets value to the instance-specific default and sets capture_time to the reset execution time.

source_refs.section: 4.3.3 Extended register (class_id = 4, version = 0)

entries.name: Demand Register
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for demand values based on averaging periods.
behavior_notes: current_average_value is the running demand over the current averaging interval.
last_average_value is calculated over the previous number_of_periods multiplied by period interval.
period and number_of_periods define the demand calculation interval.

source_refs.section: 4.3.4 Demand register (class_id = 5, version = 0)

entries.name: Register Activation
entries.definition: COSEM class used to define active register masks and register assignments.

methods.name: add_register
methods.meaning: Append one Register, Extended register, or Demand register object to register_assignment.

methods.name: add_mask
methods.meaning: Add or overwrite one activation mask in mask_list.

methods.name: delete_mask
methods.meaning: Delete one activation mask identified by mask_name.

access_semantics: register_assignment and mask_list are static configuration data; active_mask is dynamic and selects the currently enabled subset.
add_register, add_mask, and delete_mask are action methods used to maintain tariff mask configuration without rewriting the whole object.
Changing active_mask changes which assigned registers participate in the currently active tariff structure.
behavior_notes: A Register activation object groups Register, Extended register, or Demand register instances for tariff handling.
Each mask in mask_list contains indices into register_assignment; the first assigned register is referenced by index 1.
active_mask names the mask that is currently active; registers assigned to the object but not included in the active mask are disabled.
Registers not included in any Register activation object's register_assignment remain enabled by default.

source_refs.section: 4.3.5 Register activation (class_id = 6, version = 0)

entries.name: Profile Generic
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for storing captured entries such as load profiles, billing profiles, event logs, and alarm/error logs.
behavior_notes: capture_objects defines the attributes copied into the buffer by automatic capture or the capture method.
Changing capture_objects clears the buffer so all entries remain homogeneous.
capture_period 0 means no automatic capture; values >= 1 define automatic capture period in seconds.

source_refs.section: 4.3.6 Profile generic (class_id = 7, version = 1)

entries.name: Clock
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for date, time, timezone, daylight saving, and clock status.

entries.name: Script Table
entries.definition: COSEM class containing executable scripts, each composed of action specifications.

entries.name: Schedule
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for schedule tables that define scripts to be executed according to configured times or calendar conditions.

methods.name: enable_disable
methods.meaning: Disable range A and enable range B.

methods.name: insert
methods.meaning: Insert or overwrite one schedule entry by index.

methods.name: delete
methods.meaning: Delete a range of schedule entries.

access_semantics: entries is static configuration containing script logical names, script selectors, switch time, validity window, weekday masks, special-day masks, and begin/end dates.
enable_disable, insert, and delete modify schedule execution state or table contents through action methods.
A Schedule entry executes at most one script; multiple scripts at the same instant are ordered by entry index.
behavior_notes: Schedule works with Special days table to model time- and date-driven device activities.
After power failure, missed entries are processed in order when they are still within their validity_window.
Forward time changes are handled like power failure recovery; backward time changes can repeat entries during the repeated interval.
Time synchronization shall avoid losing entries or executing entries twice; daylight-saving forward intervals execute missed scripts and backward intervals suppress re-execution.

source_refs.section: 4.5.3 Schedule (class_id = 10, version = 0)

entries.name: Special Days Table
entries.definition: COSEM class containing special day definitions for tariff/calendar behavior.

entries.name: Association SN
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for short-name association objects, access rights, and xDLMS context negotiation.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the Association SN object

attributes.name: object_list
attributes.meaning: List of objects visible in the short-name association, including class, version, logical name, and short-name base

attributes.name: associated_partners_id
attributes.meaning: Client and server SAP identifiers participating in the association

attributes.name: application_context_name
attributes.meaning: xDLMS application context negotiated for the association

attributes.name: xDLMS_context_info
attributes.meaning: Negotiated conformance, PDU size, and context parameters

attributes.name: authentication_mechanism_name
attributes.meaning: Authentication mechanism used by the association

attributes.name: secret
attributes.meaning: Association secret used by authentication mechanisms

attributes.name: association_status
attributes.meaning: Current association state

attributes.name: security_setup_reference
attributes.meaning: Logical name of the related Security setup object

methods.name: reply_to_HLS_authentication
methods.meaning: Completes high-level security authentication where applicable

methods.name: change_HLS_secret
methods.meaning: Changes the association secret used by HLS mechanisms

access_semantics: Association SN controls which short-name objects and access rights are visible to a client.
The object list and access rights define the implementable data model for this client association.
Secret and authentication fields are security-sensitive and should be handled as credentials.
Security setup reference links the association to security policy, suite, and key-management configuration.

source_refs.section: 4.4.12 Association SN (class_id = 12, version = 4)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with object list, association partners, xDLMS context, authentication, HLS methods, and security setup semantics.

entries.name: Association LN
entries.definition: COSEM interface class representing a logical-name association between client and server.
access_semantics: object_list exposes visible COSEM objects together with attribute and method access rights for the association.
security_setup_reference links the association to the Security setup object that manages its security context.
association_status indicates non-associated, association-pending, or associated.

source_refs.section: 4.4.4 Association LN (class_id = 15, version = 3)

entries.name: SAP Assignment
entries.definition: COSEM interface class listing SAP assignments for logical devices.

methods.name: connect_logical_device
methods.meaning: Connect or announce one logical device SAP assignment.

methods.name: disconnect_logical_device
methods.meaning: Disconnect or remove one logical device SAP assignment.

access_semantics: SAP_assignment_list maps logical device identifiers to logical device names.
The object is typically in the management logical device when a physical device hosts more than one logical device.
Association and wrapper-port binding logic can depend on SAP assignment when resolving logical devices.
behavior_notes: SAP Assignment provides the logical-device discovery list used before clients select an application association.
In TCP-UDP/IP profiles, application processes are bound through wrapper ports and logical device SAP assignments.
ABNT Appendix 9 exposes this instance at 0-0:41.0.0.255 with SAP_assignment_list readable by all configured client classes.

source_refs.section: 4.4.5 SAP assignment (class_id = 17, version = 0)

entries.name: Image Transfer
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for transferring and activating firmware images.
behavior_notes: Image transfer is initiated, transferred block by block, verified, and then activated.
image_transferred_blocks_status tracks which blocks have already been transferred.
image_transfer_enabled controls whether the image transfer procedure is permitted.

source_refs.section: 4.4.6 Image transfer (class_id = 18, version = 0)

entries.name: IEC local port setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring IEC local port setup parameters in DLMS/COSEM devices.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the local port setup object

attributes.name: default_mode
attributes.meaning: Initial IEC local port protocol mode after opening the optical/local port

attributes.name: default_baud
attributes.meaning: Initial communication speed used before protocol negotiation

attributes.name: proposed_baud
attributes.meaning: Baud rate proposed for the negotiated communication session

attributes.name: response_time
attributes.meaning: IEC local-port response timing profile

attributes.name: device_addr
attributes.meaning: Device address used by the IEC local port protocol

attributes.name: password1
attributes.meaning: Local-port password used by lower-level IEC access where applicable

attributes.name: password2
attributes.meaning: Second local-port password used by IEC access where applicable

attributes.name: password5
attributes.meaning: Higher-level local-port password used by IEC access where applicable

access_semantics: The setup object controls local optical/IEC port negotiation and timing parameters.
Password attributes are security-sensitive and should not be logged or exported in plaintext by consuming tools.
Changing baud-rate or mode attributes can affect subsequent local communication sessions and should be treated as configuration writes.

source_refs.section: 4.4.19 IEC local port setup (class_id = 19, version = 1)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with local-port negotiation, baud-rate, timing, device address, and password semantics for engineering requirement composition.

entries.name: Activity Calendar
entries.definition: COSEM class for active and passive tariff calendars, seasons, weeks, and day profiles.

methods.name: activate_passive_calendar
methods.meaning: Copy all passive calendar attributes to their active counterparts.

access_semantics: Attributes ending in _active are the currently active calendar data.
Attributes ending in _passive are configured ahead of activation and become active through activate_passive_calendar or activate_passive_calendar_time.
Special Days table entries take precedence over Activity Calendar day-profile selection when available.
behavior_notes: Activity calendar models tariff structures using seasons, week profiles, day profiles, and script actions.
If Schedule and Activity calendar actions occur at the same activation time, Schedule-triggered actions are executed first.
After power failure only the last missed Activity calendar action is executed, and it must be ordered correctly with Schedule recovery actions.
day_profile_table entries reference Script table logical names and selectors for the actions to execute.

source_refs.section: 4.5.5 Activity calendar (class_id = 20, version = 0)

entries.name: Register monitor
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for monitoring or controlling device behavior through Register monitor functions.
access_semantics: thresholds, monitored_value, and actions are static configuration for threshold crossing behavior.
monitored_value references a simple typed attribute of Data, Register, Extended register, or Demand register.
actions has the same number of elements as thresholds; each action_set maps upward and downward threshold crossings to Script table actions.
behavior_notes: Register monitor executes scripts when the referenced value crosses configured thresholds.
The object requires a Script table instance in the same logical device.
Threshold values use the same type as the monitored attribute.

source_refs.section: 4.5.6 Register monitor (class_id = 21, version = 0)

entries.name: Single Action Schedule
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for scheduling one or more script executions at defined times.
access_semantics: executed_script references one Script table logical name and script selector.
type defines whether execution_time contains one or many entries, whether dates allow wildcards, and whether time values may differ.
execution_time stores time/date pairs; hundredths of seconds are zero for scheduled execution.
behavior_notes: Single action schedule models periodic action execution not necessarily linked to tariff calendars.
The object delegates actual behavior to the referenced Script table action.
Types 1 through 5 constrain the execution_time cardinality, wildcard use, and whether time values may vary.

source_refs.section: 4.5.7 Single action schedule (class_id = 22, version = 0)

entries.name: IEC HDLC Setup
entries.definition: COSEM class for IEC HDLC communication setup parameters.
access_semantics: All IEC HDLC setup attributes are static channel configuration parameters.
comm_speed enumerates 300 through 115200 baud values and may be overridden by entering HDLC mode through another protocol.
window sizes and maximum information field lengths may be negotiated to smaller values during logon.
behavior_notes: IEC HDLC setup configures one communication channel according to IEC 62056-46.
inter_octet_time_out defines when received octets are treated as a complete frame.
inactivity_time_out defines disconnection processing when no frame is received; zero disables the timeout.
device_address contains the physical HDLC device address with reserved, calling, and broadcast ranges.

source_refs.section: 4.7.2 IEC HDLC setup (class_id = 23, version = 1)
5.7.2 IEC HDLC setup (class_id = 23, version = 0)

entries.name: IEC twisted pair (1) setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring IEC twisted pair (1) setup parameters in DLMS/COSEM devices.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the IEC twisted-pair setup object

attributes.name: mode
attributes.meaning: Operating mode of the IEC twisted-pair communication port

attributes.name: speed
attributes.meaning: Configured communication speed for the twisted-pair link

attributes.name: primary_address
attributes.meaning: Primary address used on the IEC twisted-pair bus

attributes.name: tabis
attributes.meaning: Timing parameter used by IEC twisted-pair communication

access_semantics: The setup object defines wired IEC twisted-pair communication parameters, including mode, speed, and bus addressing.
Address and speed writes affect physical-link interoperability and should be validated against the meter profile before applying.
Timing parameters should be treated as protocol configuration rather than metering data.

source_refs.section: 4.4.24 IEC twisted pair (1) setup (class_id = 24, version = 1)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with wired IEC mode, speed, address, and timing semantics.

entries.name: M-Bus slave port setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring the M-Bus slave port of a DLMS/COSEM device, including baud-rate capabilities and slave address state.

attributes.name: default_baud
attributes.meaning: Default communication speed used by the M-Bus slave port

attributes.name: available_baud
attributes.meaning: Baud rates supported by the physical M-Bus slave port

attributes.name: address_state
attributes.meaning: State of the M-Bus slave addressing procedure

attributes.name: bus_address
attributes.meaning: Configured M-Bus slave address

access_semantics: The setup object configures local M-Bus slave communication parameters rather than metrology values.
default_baud and bus_address are implementation configuration points and should be protected by the management access profile.
available_baud and address_state are read-only diagnostic or capability attributes.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention M-Bus slave port speed, supported baud rates, bus address assignment, or slave address state.
It complements M-Bus client/master setup classes; this object represents the local slave-side port setup.

source_refs.section: 4.7.6 M-Bus slave port setup (class_id = 25, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with M-Bus slave port baud-rate and address-state semantics for communication setup requirements.

entries.name: Utility tables
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for representing compact utility-defined tables whose cells are addressed through active and passive axes.

attributes.name: table_cell_values
attributes.meaning: Table payload values arranged according to the active and passive axis definitions

attributes.name: table_active_axis
attributes.meaning: Definition of the active axis used to address or interpret rows, columns, or intervals

attributes.name: table_passive_axis
attributes.meaning: Definition of the passive axis used together with the active axis to interpret table cells

access_semantics: table_cell_values is the main data payload and should be interpreted only with the matching active and passive axis definitions.
Axis attributes are configuration data; changing them changes the meaning of the stored table values and should be restricted to authorized clients.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention utility-defined tables, tabular measurement data, active axis, passive axis, or addressed table cells.
Requirements involving Utility tables should preserve both table value constraints and axis interpretation constraints; a cell value alone is usually insufficient for implementation.

source_refs.section: 4.3.7 Utility tables (class_id = 26, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with table payload, axis configuration, and implementation semantics.

entries.name: Modem configuration
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring modem communication profiles and initialization behavior.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the modem configuration object

attributes.name: comm_speed
attributes.meaning: Configured modem communication speed

attributes.name: initialization_string
attributes.meaning: Command string used to initialize the modem before communication

attributes.name: modem_profile
attributes.meaning: Profile of modem initialization and communication settings

access_semantics: The object stores modem initialization and speed parameters used before establishing a communication session.
Initialization strings are configuration commands and may contain vendor-specific modem control sequences.
Profile changes can alter remote communication availability and should be reviewed as communication configuration.

source_refs.section: 4.4.27 Modem configuration (class_id = 27, version = 1)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with modem speed, initialization string, and profile semantics.

entries.name: Auto answer
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for controlling how a device answers incoming communication calls.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the auto answer object

attributes.name: mode
attributes.meaning: Auto-answer operating mode

attributes.name: listening_window
attributes.meaning: Time windows during which the device is allowed to answer incoming calls

attributes.name: status
attributes.meaning: Current auto-answer status

attributes.name: number_of_calls
attributes.meaning: Number of answered incoming calls or calls tracked by the auto-answer function

attributes.name: number_of_rings
attributes.meaning: Number of rings before the device answers

access_semantics: The auto answer object determines whether and when the meter accepts incoming communication sessions.
Listening-window configuration is time-dependent behavior and should be validated with clock/time-zone assumptions.
Ring and call counters support diagnostics but should not be confused with metering event logs.

source_refs.section: 4.4.28 Auto answer (class_id = 28, version = 2)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with incoming-call mode, listening-window, status, calls, and ring semantics.

entries.name: Auto connect
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for controlling automatic outgoing communication attempts.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the auto connect object

attributes.name: mode
attributes.meaning: Auto-connect operating mode

attributes.name: repetitions
attributes.meaning: Number of repeated outgoing connection attempts

attributes.name: repetition_delay
attributes.meaning: Delay between repeated connection attempts

attributes.name: calling_window
attributes.meaning: Time windows during which automatic outgoing calls are permitted

attributes.name: destinations
attributes.meaning: Destination endpoints or identifiers used for outgoing connections

access_semantics: The auto connect object controls scheduled or triggered outgoing communication attempts.
Calling windows and repetition parameters define retry behavior and should be treated as operational communication policy.
Destination entries may include addresses or endpoint identifiers and should be validated before deployment.

source_refs.section: 4.4.29 Auto connect (class_id = 29, version = 2)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with outgoing-call mode, retry, calling-window, and destination semantics.

entries.name: COSEM data protection
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for binding COSEM data to protection metadata, protection parameters, and status used by secure data handling.

attributes.name: protection_buffer
attributes.meaning: Protected payload or protected representation of the associated COSEM data

attributes.name: protection_parameters
attributes.meaning: Parameters controlling how the protected data is generated, verified, or interpreted

attributes.name: protection_status
attributes.meaning: Current status of the protected data object and its protection process

methods.name: protect
methods.meaning: Trigger generation or update of protected data when supported by the implementation

methods.name: verify
methods.meaning: Verify protected data or protection metadata when supported by the implementation

access_semantics: protection_buffer is security-sensitive output and should be exposed only according to the configured association and security policy.
protection_parameters control security behavior and should require management-level write privileges.
Protection methods, when available, are active operations and should be treated as high-risk operations in requirements review.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention protected COSEM data, data protection status, cryptographic protection parameters, or protected payload verification.
This class belongs with security and access-control requirements, not ordinary measurement display requirements.

behavior_notes: push_object_list defines the COSEM object attributes sent when push is invoked.
Push is sent using the DataNotification service when configured trigger conditions are met.
communication_window, randomisation_start_interval, number_of_retries, and repetition_delay define delayed execution and retry behavior.
push_protection_parameters define data protection options for pushed data.

source_refs.section: 4.4.8.2 Push setup (class_id = 40, version = 3)

entries.name: TCP-UDP Setup
entries.definition: COSEM class for TCP/UDP transport setup parameters.
access_semantics: TCP_UDP_port defines the TCP/UDP or CoAP UDP endpoint port used by the physical device.
IP_reference points to the IPv4 or IPv6 setup object that owns IP-layer address settings.
MSS is advertised only in the initial TCP SYN and is not negotiated by this attribute.
behavior_notes: TCP-UDP setup models the TCP or UDP sub-layer for TCP-UDP/IP and CoAP communication profiles.
Within a physical device, each client AP or server logical device is bound to a wrapper port with help from SAP Assignment.
nb_of_sim_conn limits simultaneous TCP/UDP connections supported by the transport layer.
inactivity_time_out aborts inactive TCP connections; zero disables this timeout.

source_refs.section: 4.9.1 TCP-UDP setup (class_id = 41, version = 0)

entries.name: IPv4 Setup
entries.definition: COSEM class for IPv4 network setup parameters.

methods.name: add_mc_IP_address
methods.meaning: Add one multicast IPv4 address.

methods.name: delete_mc_IP_address
methods.meaning: Delete one multicast IPv4 address.

methods.name: get_nbof_mc_IP_addresses
methods.meaning: Return the number of configured multicast IPv4 addresses.

access_semantics: DL_reference points to the lower data-link setup object used by this IPv4 interface.
IP_address may be static or dynamic; when no address is assigned the value is zero.
multicast_IP_address holds class-D multicast addresses accepted by the device.
use_DHCP_flag controls dynamic IP assignment, while DNS and gateway attributes hold resulting network parameters.
behavior_notes: A device has one IPv4 setup instance for each network interface that uses TCP-UDP/IPv4.
IPv4 addresses are encoded as double-long-unsigned values, for example 192.168.0.1 as C0A80001.
IP_options may carry RFC 791 options such as security, source route, record route, or internet timestamp.
